div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother church, present and future, Richard of Lincoln sends greeting. Know me to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, for the souls of my father and my mother and of all my ancestors and successors, and for the salvation of my lord, King William, and of the queen and their son Alexander, and of Earl David, and for my own salvation and my wife's and all our heirs' salvation, in pure and everlasting alms, the portion of land in the territory of Mow that Anselm of Whitton gave to the same church and monks with the adjacent meadows, to be held and possessed for ever by all the borders and boundaries contained in the charter of the said Anslem. In addition I have given and confirmed the whole peatery that is between Mowhope and Berhope and 'Herdstrete' which divides the land of Mow and of Hownam. I have moreover given them in my wood of Mowhope as much firewood as one horse can carry to Hownam Grange, each year from Easter to the Nativity of Saint Mary. I will that the said monks hold and possess all these things for ever as freely and quitely and honourably as they hold and possess any other alms freely and quitely, fully and honourably. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the said monks this alms against all men, and dishcarge it of forinsec and every earthly service in respect of all men for ever. And the monks shall have free and appropriate entrance and exit through my land for fetching their firewood and peats and other necessities without disturbance and hindrance. With these witnesses: Gervase Avenel; William Avenel; William of Cresswell; William de Crachin; Alan the clerk; Walter of Ryedale; Robert of Barnoldby.
